name: CALM Hub Architecture
description: Architecture of the CALM Hub system, comprising a React UI, a Quarkus REST API backend, a MongoDB database, and an optional Keycloak OIDC identity provider.

nodes.name: CALM Hub UI
nodes.description: React single-page application (Vite) that allows users to browse namespaces, patterns, architectures, flows, standards, controls, and decorators stored in the CALM Hub.

nodes.name: CALM Hub API
nodes.description: Quarkus JAX-RS REST backend exposing the /calm/** namespace. Serves patterns, architectures, flows, standards, controls, schemas, domains, decorators, ADRs, and user-access resources.

nodes.name: MongoDB
nodes.description: MongoDB instance used as the primary persistent storage backend for CALM Hub (database: calmSchemas).

nodes.name: Architect
nodes.description: A human architect who uses the CALM Hub UI to browse, create, and manage CALM artefacts such as patterns, architectures, and flows.

nodes.name: Keycloak
nodes.description: OIDC identity provider for CALM Hub. Used when the secure profile is enabled; the UI performs OIDC sign-in redirects and the API validates JWT tokens against this realm.

relationships.description: The Architect interacts with the CALM Hub UI to browse and manage CALM artefacts.
The CALM Hub UI calls the CALM Hub REST API over HTTP(S) to fetch and display namespaces, patterns, architectures, flows, standards, controls, and decorators.
The CALM Hub API persists and retrieves all CALM artefacts (patterns, architectures, flows, etc.) from MongoDB.
When OIDC authentication is enabled, the CALM Hub UI redirects users to Keycloak for sign-in using the authorisation-code flow.
When the secure profile is active, the CALM Hub API validates inbound JWT bearer tokens against the Keycloak realm.
